Title: Tn-seq profiling reveals that NodS of the beta-rhizobium Paraburkholderia phymatum is detrimental for nodulating soybean

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Glycine max; Phaseolus vulgaris; Mimosa pudica
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Nodulation factor N-methylation / N-methylated nodulation factors
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines rhizobium-legume host specificity and promotes nodulation signaling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Rhizobial extracellular nodulation factors in the rhizosphere at the root interface
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates the non-reducing terminal N-deacetylated chitooligosaccharide intermediate during nodulation factor biosynthesis before NodA-mediated acylation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: N-methylated chitooligosaccharide / N-methylated nodulation factor precursor
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Strain-specific nodulation factor decoration module (N-methylation branch)
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: downstream modification step during NF assembly
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key determinant of host-specific NF structure; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Presence of nodS prevents P. phymatum from nodulating soybean, supports optimal nodulation of common bean, and is dispensable for nodulation of Mimosa pudica
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tn-seq gain-of-function screen; targeted deletion mutant; genetic complementation in trans; in planta nodulation assays; nitrogenase activity assay; histology/TEM
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tn-seq of soybean nodules identified repeated insertions in nodS as the major mutation associated with gain of nodulation on soybean. 2. A nodS deletion mutant formed nodules on soybean whereas wild type and complemented strain did not, demonstrating that intact nodS is detrimental for soybean nodulation. 3. In common bean, nodS absence reduced nodule number but increased nodule dry weight and nitrogenase activity. 4. In Mimosa pudica, nodS was dispensable for nodulation and symbiotic efficiency.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Previously reported in discussion as upregulated in P. phymatum exposed to Mimosa pudica root exudates
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Plant host context: soybean, common bean, or Mimosa pudica inoculation; root exudate context discussed for Mimosa pudica
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Complementation from pBBR1MCS2, transcription driven by T7 promoter
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Tn-seq / transposon sequencing; raw FASTQ in NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA1089526
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Editing or deleting nodS in P. phymatum-like inoculant strains could broaden compatibility to soybean, but host-specific tradeoffs must be considered because nodS also improves nodulation output in common bean. Deployment should be host-tailored rather than universal.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion enables soybean nodulation but nodules show lower nitrogenase activity than Bradyrhizobium diazoefficiens and signs of premature senescence; in common bean, nodS deletion reduces nodule number despite increasing per-nodule nitrogenase activity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper infers NodS function from annotation and prior rhizobial literature; actual NF chemical structures in P. phymatum wild type versus nodS mutant were not determined in this study.
